obligation: Ensure strict client-level segregation of advisory and distribution activities at the group or family level, preventing existing advisory clients from availing distribution services, and vice versa.
evidence_required: Client onboarding database, system controls, and signed client declarations.

obligation: Provide new clients with a clear option to choose either advisory or distribution services within the group/family at the time of onboarding.
evidence_required: Onboarding forms or electronic selection record.

obligation: Maintain and use the Permanent Account Number (PAN) of each client as the primary control record for identification and segregation of clients.
evidence_required: PAN verification records and client database audit logs.

obligation: Reckon the family of an individual client as a single client and use the PAN of all family members jointly and severally as the control record.
evidence_required: Family mapping declarations and mapped PAN record database.

obligation: Obtain an annual declaration or periodic update from the client regarding dependent family members whose assets originate from the earning individual client.
evidence_required: Signed annual declaration form from client.

obligation: Advise direct plans (non-commission based) of products only, wherever such plans are available.
evidence_required: Written recommendation records and investment advice logs.

obligation: Obtain an annual compliance audit certificate from an auditor (for individual IA) or statutory auditor (for non-individual IA) confirming compliance with client level segregation requirements.
deadline: Within 6 months from the end of the financial year
evidence_required: Annual compliance audit report and auditor's certificate.

obligation: Enter into a formal investment advisory agreement with clients mandatorily covering all the terms and conditions provided in Annexure A.
deadline: Prior to rendering any investment advice or charging any fees
evidence_required: Executed Investment Advisory Agreement signed by both parties.

obligation: Charge fees from clients strictly under either Assets under Advice (AUA) mode (capped at 2.5% p.a. per client) or Fixed fee mode (capped at INR 1,25,000 p.a. per client).
evidence_required: Fee invoices, client ledger, and advisory agreements.

obligation: Demonstrate the client's Assets under Advice (AUA) using supporting documents like demat statements or unit statements of the client.
evidence_required: Demat statements, unit statements, and fee calculation sheets.

obligation: Deduct any portion of AUA held by the client under pre-existing distribution arrangements from the total AUA for charging advisory fees.
evidence_required: Demat portfolio holding breakdown and advisory fee computation reports.

obligation: Implement changes in fee modes only after a minimum period of 12 months from onboarding or the last change of fee mode.
deadline: After 12 months of on boarding/last change of mode
evidence_required: Client agreement addendums and billing records audit trail.

obligation: Limit advance fee collections to a maximum of 2 quarters.
evidence_required: Fee receipt vouchers and bank credit advice.

obligation: Refund the fee for the unexpired period in the event of pre-mature termination of services, retaining a maximum breakage fee of not more than one quarter's fee.
evidence_required: Refund payment proof, calculation sheet, and termination notice copy.

obligation: Comply with qualification and certification requirements under Regulation 7 of IA Regulations (with specific NISM certifications required for individual IAs above fifty years of age).
evidence_required: NISM certificates, educational degrees, and birth/age proof documents.

obligation: An individual IA must apply for registration as a non-individual investment adviser in Form-A on or before reaching 150 clients.
evidence_required: Submitted Form-A application copy and fee payment receipt.

obligation: Cease onboarding fresh clients once the client count reaches 150 until the grant of registration as a non-individual IA by SEBI.
deadline: Upon reaching 150 clients and until registration is granted
evidence_required: Client registration logs and compliance reports.

obligation: Maintain records of interactions with clients and prospective clients where advisory conversation has taken place, including physical written records, telephone recordings, emails, and SMS.
deadline: From first interaction until the completion of advisory services
evidence_required: Call recording files, email archives, SMS logs, and physical records register.

obligation: Preserve all client interaction and advisory records for a minimum period of five years (or longer if a dispute has been raised).
deadline: For a period of 5 years (or until dispute resolution)
evidence_required: Archival databases, record retention policy, and storage logs.

obligation: Ensure that an annual audit of compliance with IA regulations and circulars is conducted and completed.
deadline: Within six months from the end of each financial year
evidence_required: Annual audit report issued by a chartered accountant or company secretary.

obligation: Report the adverse findings of the annual audit and approved action taken to the respective SEBI office.
deadline: Within one month of the audit report and not later than October 31st of each year
evidence_required: Submission acknowledgement from SEBI office.

obligation: Use the investment policy approved by the board or management team of non-individual clients to perform risk profiling and suitability analysis.
deadline: Prior to onboarding and advising non-individual clients
evidence_required: Risk assessment report mapping to the non-individual client's approved investment policy.

obligation: Prominently display the registered name, registration type, number, complete address, contact details of Principal Officer, and SEBI regional office address on websites, mobile apps, printed/electronic materials, KYC forms, and client agreements.
evidence_required: Website homepage screenshot, copies of KYC forms, and agreements.

obligation: Do not provide free trial of any products/services to prospective clients, and do not accept any part payments in advance.
evidence_required: Fee schedule, marketing materials, and billing records.

obligation: Provide investment advice only after completing the client's risk profile and obtaining their consent on the completed risk profile via registered email or physical document.
evidence_required: Risk profiling questionnaires and written/email consent records.

obligation: Accept fees strictly through banking channels (account payee crossed cheques, demand drafts, or direct credit via NEFT/RTGS/IMPS/UPI) and never accept cash deposits.
evidence_required: Bank statements and receipts showing compliance with acceptable modes.

obligation: Display the monthly update of investor complaints status on the homepage (without scrolling) of the website or mobile app in the format specified in Annexure C.
deadline: By the 7th of the succeeding month
evidence_required: Dated screenshot of the homepage showing the compliant complaints table.

obligation: Seek and maintain membership of the designated Investment Adviser Administration and Supervisory Body (IAASB) to keep registration in force.
evidence_required: Membership certificate issued by IAASB (such as BASL).

obligation: Submit periodic reports to the designated IAASB as specified by them.
evidence_required: Report submission receipts and copies of submitted reports.

obligation: Ensure that critical and compliance data remains within the legal boundary of India when employing Software as a Service (SaaS) based solutions.
evidence_required: SaaS service agreement and server hosting location details.

obligation: Submit a half-yearly undertaking to SEBI confirming compliance with the advisory for SaaS-based solutions.
evidence_required: Submitted half-yearly compliance undertaking.

obligation: Display information about the grievance redressal mechanism and SCORES platform prominently in the IA's physical offices.
evidence_required: Photographs of the notice board inside the office premises.

obligation: Prominently display the Investor Charter on websites and mobile applications (or send via email as a one-time measure if no website/app exists).
evidence_required: Website screenshot or email transmission records of the Investor Charter.

obligation: Obtain prior approval of SEBI through the online SI Portal in case of any change in control of the Investment Adviser.
deadline: Prior to effecting any change in control
evidence_required: SEBI approval letter/communication.

obligation: Submit an application for fresh registration to SEBI within six months from the date of receiving prior approval for a change in control.
deadline: Within six months of change in control approval
evidence_required: SI Portal application confirmation receipt.

obligation: Ensure that prior approval for all advertisements and materials is obtained from a SEBI-recognized supervisory body (such as BASL) before issue.
deadline: Prior to issuance of any advertisement
evidence_required: Approval certificate/letter issued by BASL.

obligation: Include the mandatory standard warning in legible fonts (minimum font size 10) in all advertisements: 'Investment in securities market are subject to market risks. Read all the related documents carefully before investing.'
evidence_required: Copies of published advertisements and materials.

obligation: Do not include the SEBI logo in any advertisements or communication material.
evidence_required: Published advertisement copies and marketing materials.

obligation: Retain a copy of all published advertisements for a period of five years.
deadline: For a period of 5 years from issuance date
evidence_required: Advertisement registers and archival records.

obligation: Establish internal controls to ensure employees do not encourage or circulate unverified market rumors or unauthenticated news.
evidence_required: Internal policy document on rumor control and training attendance logs.

obligation: Do not outsource core business activities (such as risk profiling, suitability analysis, and providing investment advice) and compliance functions.
evidence_required: SOPs, employee definitions, and third-party vendor agreements.

obligation: Implement policies and internal procedures to identify, avoid, or manage actual or potential conflicts of interest, developed under the active involvement of senior management.
evidence_required: Board-approved Conflict of Interest Policy.

obligation: Ensure compliance with Anti-Money Laundering (AML) standards and Combating the Financing of Terrorism (CFT) as mandated under the PMLA rules.
evidence_required: PMLA compliance manual, customer verification records, and audit reports.

obligation: Ensure client level segregation of advisory and distribution activities at the group or family level, preventing advisory clients from receiving distribution services and vice versa.
evidence_required: Client database system controls, onboarding declaration records, and separate ledger audits.

obligation: Provide new clients with a explicit option to choose either advisory or distribution services within the group/family at the time of onboarding.
evidence_required: Client selection declaration forms or electronic audit trails of onboarding choice.

obligation: Utilize the Permanent Account Number (PAN) of each client as the unique control record for identification and segregation of client accounts.
evidence_required: PAN registry and verified database of clients mapped with their segregation status.

obligation: Treat the family of an individual client as a single client and use the PAN of all family members jointly and severally as the control record for identification.
evidence_required: Family identification mapping declarations and joint PAN record logs.

obligation: Obtain an annual declaration or periodic update from the client regarding dependent family members whose assets originate from the earning individual client.
evidence_required: Signed annual declaration forms or updated electronic records.

obligation: Advise direct plans (non-commission based) of products only, wherever such plans are available in the market.
evidence_required: Investment recommendation reports and compliance system parameter configurations.

obligation: Obtain an annual compliance audit certificate from an auditor (for individual IA) or statutory auditor (for non-individual IA) confirming client-level segregation.
deadline: Within 6 months from the end of the financial year
evidence_required: Annual audit report containing the compliance certificate.

obligation: Enter into a formal investment advisory agreement with clients mandatorily covering all terms and conditions specified in Annexure A.
deadline: Prior to rendering any advisory service or charging any fees
evidence_required: Fully signed copy of the Investment Advisory Agreement.

obligation: Charge fees under either Assets under Advice (AUA) mode (capped at 2.5% p.a. per client) or Fixed fee mode (capped at INR 1,25,000 p.a. per client).
evidence_required: Advisory agreement and invoices raised matching SEBI limits.

obligation: Demonstrate the AUA figures with appropriate supporting documents such as demat statements or unit statements of the client.
evidence_required: Client demat and mutual fund unit statements matching the billing audit trail.

obligation: Deduct assets held by the client under pre-existing distribution arrangements with any entity from the total AUA for fee calculations.
evidence_required: Asset distribution history logs and adjusted fee calculations.

obligation: Ensure that the selected fee mode (AUA or Fixed) is run on an annual basis and not changed before 12 months from onboarding or last change.
deadline: Minimum 12 months interval for fee mode change
evidence_required: System audits tracking client agreement dates and billing parameters.

obligation: Limit advance fee collection to a maximum of two quarters.
evidence_required: Receipt logs, invoices, and bank account ledgers.

obligation: Refund the fee for the unexpired period in the event of pre-mature termination of services, retaining a maximum breakage fee of one quarter.
evidence_required: Refund calculations, payment vouchers, and written termination records.

obligation: Comply with the enhanced qualification and experience requirements under Regulation 7(1) of the IA Regulations on or before the extended timeline.
evidence_required: Professional certificates, NISM certifications, and qualification assessment records.

obligation: An individual IA must apply for registration as a non-individual investment adviser in Form-A on or before reaching 150 clients.
evidence_required: Submitted Form-A copy and registration application trace on SEBI SI Portal.

obligation: Stop onboarding fresh clients once client count reaches 150, until the grant of registration as a non-individual IA by SEBI.
deadline: Immediately upon reaching 150 clients until registered
evidence_required: Client database lists and onboarding chronological logs.

obligation: Maintain interactive conversation records with all clients and prospective clients regarding advisory service interactions (including written, email, SMS, and telephone logs).
deadline: From first interaction until completion of services
evidence_required: Archived voice recordings, system-saved email databases, and physical logs.

obligation: Retain interaction and advisory records for a minimum period of five years, keeping them longer if a regulatory dispute is open.
deadline: For a minimum of 5 years from conversation date
evidence_required: System backup logs, cloud archival verification, and compliance registers.

obligation: Conduct an annual compliance audit of the IA operations by an external chartered accountant or company secretary.
deadline: Within six months of the end of each financial year
evidence_required: Signed Annual Audit Report and SEBI filing records.

obligation: Report the adverse findings of the annual compliance audit along with action taken to the respective SEBI local/regional office.
deadline: Within 1 month of audit report and no later than October 31st
evidence_required: Filing acknowledgment receipts from SEBI.

obligation: Use the board-approved investment policy of a non-individual client to perform their risk profiling and suitability analysis.
evidence_required: Copy of client's board-approved investment policy and matching suitability report.

obligation: Display registration numbers, address, principal officer contact, and corresponding SEBI local address prominently on websites, mobile apps, printed materials, and client communications.
evidence_required: Website/app screenshots, correspondence templates, and print copies.

obligation: Do not offer free trials of products/services to prospective clients, and do not accept any partial payments in advance.
evidence_required: Invoicing trails, standard marketing policy documents, and website checks.

obligation: Obtain consent from the client on the completed risk profile via registered email or physical document before providing advice.
evidence_required: Signed risk profiling document or email confirmation thread on record.

obligation: Accept fee payments strictly through banking channels using account payee crossed cheques, demand drafts, or direct bank transfer, rejecting cash deposits.
evidence_required: Bank reconciliation records and account ledgers.

obligation: Prominently display the monthly update of investor complaints on the homepage (without scrolling) of websites and mobile apps using font size 12 or above.
deadline: By the 7th of the succeeding month
evidence_required: Dated homepage screenshot verifying the complaint table placement and size.

obligation: Acquire and maintain enlistment with the designated Stock Exchange recognized as IAASB to keep registration valid.
evidence_required: Active enlistment certificate or notification from IAASB.

obligation: Submit periodic reports to the recognized IAASB in the format and manner specified under the operational guidelines.
evidence_required: Confirmation filings and acknowledgement records from the IAASB platform.

obligation: Ensure complete control and security of governance, risk, and compliance data by retaining critical data within the geographical boundaries of India when using SaaS systems.
evidence_required: SaaS contract specifications and localization audits.

obligation: Submit half-yearly compliance undertakings regarding the use of Software as a Service (SaaS) based solutions directly to SEBI.
evidence_required: Submitted SaaS advisory undertaking in the specified format.

obligation: Prominently display grievance redressal options including Compliance Officer details, SEBI SCORES portal link, and Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) links on platforms and in offices.
evidence_required: Office display photographs and website footer/contact links.

obligation: Display the SEBI Investor Charter prominently on websites and mobile applications (or email to registered addresses if no active website/app is used).
evidence_required: Website charter screenshots or bulk email transmission records.

obligation: Obtain SEBI's prior written approval via the SI Portal prior to executing any transition of change in control of the Investment Adviser.
deadline: Before change in control is executed
evidence_required: Prior approval order or letter issued by SEBI.

obligation: Obtain mandatory approval from the recognized supervisory body (BSE Administration & Supervision Ltd.) before releasing any marketing or advertisement materials.
evidence_required: Approval certificate/letter issued by BASL/IAASB.

obligation: Incorporate standard risk warnings in minimum 10-point font size in all advertisement communications without altering or deleting the designated wording.
evidence_required: Copies of published advertisements.

obligation: Do not include the SEBI logo in any advertisements, publications, or client communications.
evidence_required: Published advertisement and correspondence copies.

obligation: Retain a hard or digital copy of all issued advertisements and BASL approvals for a period of five years.
deadline: 5 years from date of advertisement publication
evidence_required: Compliance folders and retrieval registers.

obligation: Implement internal system controls and codes of conduct to ensure employees do not share unverified rumors, news, or unauthenticated information.
evidence_required: Employee Code of Conduct and internal monitoring logs.

obligation: Do not outsource core advisory functions, risk profiling, suitability determinations, and compliance-related responsibilities to third parties.
evidence_required: Standard operating procedures and third-party vendor agreements.

obligation: Establish policies and procedures with active board and senior management input to identify, monitor, and prevent actual or potential conflicts of interest.
evidence_required: Board-approved Conflict of Interest Policy.

obligation: Comply with unified Know Your Client (KYC) norms as specified in the SEBI Master Circular dated October 12, 2023.
evidence_required: Client KYC files, verification records, and automated check parameters.

obligation: Apply the simplified requirements for the grant of accreditation to investors as per SEBI guidelines.
evidence_required: Verification documentation, client onboarding records, and accredited investor logs.

obligation: Submit a standardized periodic report in the format specified in Annexure H on a half-yearly basis to the IAASB.
deadline: For half-yearly periods ending September 30 and March 31 of every financial year
evidence_required: Filing trace, digital receipt, and submitted Annexure H document copy.

obligation: Ensure that the periodic reports are filed with IAASB within seven working days from the end of each half-yearly period.
deadline: Within seven working days from the end of the half-yearly period
evidence_required: Dated submission timestamp and digital acknowledgment from IAASB portal.

obligation: Ensure strict client-level segregation of advisory and distribution activities at the group or family level, preventing existing advisory clients from receiving distribution services and vice versa.
evidence_required: Client onboarding logs, separate database configurations, and signed segregation declarations.

obligation: Provide new clients with a clear option to choose either advisory or distribution services within the group/family at the time of onboarding.
evidence_required: Onboarding forms or electronic audit trails of choice selection.

obligation: Use the Permanent Account Number (PAN) of each client as the primary control record for identification and client-level segregation.
evidence_required: Verified PAN records, client profiles, and compliance database entries.

obligation: Treat the family of an individual client as a single client, using the PAN of all family members jointly and severally as the control record.
evidence_required: Family declarations and mapped PAN record database.

obligation: Obtain an annual declaration or periodic update from the client in respect of dependent family members whose assets originate from the earning individual client.
evidence_required: Signed annual declaration form from the client.

obligation: Advise direct plans (non-commission based) of products only, wherever such plans are available.
evidence_required: Investment recommendation records and advisory logs.

obligation: Obtain an annual compliance audit certificate from an auditor confirming compliance with client level segregation requirements.
deadline: Within 6 months from the end of the financial year
evidence_required: Annual compliance audit report and auditor's segregation certificate.

obligation: Sign a standard waiver if providing investment advisory services exclusively to institutional clients and accredited investors to remain exempt from segregation requirements.
deadline: At onboarding of exclusive institutional/accredited clients
evidence_required: Signed standard waiver from the client on record.

obligation: Incorporate Standardized Most Important Terms and Conditions (MITC) as provided in Annexure B into the mandatory client investment advisory agreement.
deadline: Prior to rendering advice/charging fees (By June 30, 2025 for existing clients as of Feb 17, 2025)
evidence_required: Executed agreement incorporating Annexure B MITC or email proof for existing clients.

obligation: Obtain client consent/agreement signatures in person or via other legally acceptable modes including DigiLocker-enabled Aadhaar based e-signatures.
deadline: Prior to rendering any advice or charging any fees
evidence_required: Digitally signed or physically signed agreement copy with timestamp audit trail.

obligation: Provide standard guidance to clients in the agreement on the optional Centralised Fee Collection Mechanism (CeFCoM).
evidence_required: Client agreement copy showing CeFCoM guidance section.

obligation: Limit fees for individual and HUF clients to either Assets under Advice (AUA) mode (max 2.5% of AUA p.a. per family of client) or Fixed fee mode (max ₹ 1,51,000 p.a. per family of client).
evidence_required: Client invoices, ledger, and agreement fee schedules.

obligation: Ensure that the aggregate NAV of securities under the purview of SEBI is used exclusively for charging fees under the AUA mode.
evidence_required: Calculations showing non-SEBI asset exclusion and demat holding sheets.

obligation: Limit advance fee collection, if agreed by the client, to a maximum of one year.
evidence_required: Advisory agreement and billing receipt registers.

obligation: Maintain a baseline security deposit based on previous financial year max client count: ₹ 1L (up to 150 clients), ₹ 2L (151-300), ₹ 5L (301-1000), or ₹ 10L (1001+).
deadline: Latest by 30th April of the subsequent FY (Existing IAs comply by Sept 30, 2025)
evidence_required: Lien letter marked in favor of IAASB with a scheduled bank.

obligation: Comply with revised qualification criteria for individual investment advisers, principal officers, and associated persons as relaxed by SEBI (Second Amendment) Regulations, 2024.
evidence_required: Educational degrees and active NISM certification credentials.

obligation: Apply for in-principle registration as a non-individual IA if client count exceeds 300 at any point of time or fee collected exceeds ₹ 3 crores during the financial year.
deadline: Whichever trigger is earlier, completing transition within 3 months of in-principle approval
evidence_required: Submitted Form-A and in-principle approval trace on SI Portal.

obligation: For part-time IAs, ensure that investment advisory services are clearly segregated from other businesses, and maintain arms-length relationships.
evidence_required: Signed arms-length undertaking, separate operations manuals.

obligation: For part-time IAs, provide a prominent disclaimer (minimum 10 font size) on non-SEBI service invoices indicating services are not under SEBI purview and no recourse is available.
deadline: At time of invoicing non-SEBI services
evidence_required: Copies of non-SEBI invoices showing mandatory disclaimer.

obligation: A partnership firm registered as an IA, where no partner has minimum qualifications, must apply for registration as an LLP or body corporate.
evidence_required: Registration transformation applications submitted to MCA and SEBI.

obligation: For products and services not under SEBI purview, make written disclosures and obtain signed client undertakings declaring no SEBI recourse is available.
deadline: At onboarding of client (by July 31, 2025 for existing clients as of Jan 8, 2025)
evidence_required: Signed client undertaking on record.

obligation: Disclose to the client the extent of use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools in providing investment advice at the time of entering the agreement and as required.
deadline: At time of entering into agreement
evidence_required: Standard client agreements containing AI disclosures.

obligation: Maintain time-stamped call recordings of every consent for implementation/execution service obtained via telephone from advisory clients.
evidence_required: Time-stamped audio call logs linked to client execution ledger.

obligation: Conduct an annual audit of compliance with SEBI Regulations and specify compliance status on each provision in the annual audit report.
deadline: Within six months from the end of each financial year
evidence_required: Comprehensive annual compliance audit report specifying each provision.

obligation: Publish the compliance audit status and adverse findings, if any, along with action taken on the IA website and provide the report to clients.
deadline: Starting from audit report for financial year ending March 31, 2025
evidence_required: Website screenshots of audit reports and records of reports sent to clients.

obligation: Maintain a functional website containing details as specified by SEBI and confirm its details to IAASB.
deadline: By June 30, 2025 for existing IAs as on Jan 8, 2025
evidence_required: Active website domain and written confirmation receipt from IAASB.

obligation: Display updated investor complaints data on the homepage (without scrolling) of websites/mobile apps by the 7th of the succeeding month using font size 12 or above.
deadline: By the 7th of the succeeding month
evidence_required: Dated screenshots of the complaints table on the homepage.

obligation: Bring the updated SEBI Investor Charter to the notice of clients through website display, prominent office placement, and client onboarding kits.
evidence_required: Website charter screenshot, physical verification in offices, and copy of onboarding emails.

obligation: Ensure that any past performance or risk-return metric advertisements are verified by SEBI-recognized Past Risk and Return Verification Agency (PaRRVA).
evidence_required: Verification report from PaRRVA and approved advertisement layouts.

obligation: Submit standardized periodic reports in the format specified in Annexure H on a half-yearly basis to IAASB within 30 days from period end.
deadline: Within 30 days of period ending Sept 30 and March 31 (March 31, 2025 extended to July 31, 2025)
evidence_required: Filing trace and receipt from IAASB portal under Annexure H.

obligation: Ensure client level segregation of advisory and distribution activities at the group/family level, preventing advisory clients from receiving distribution services and vice versa.
evidence_required: Client onboarding database, system segregation rules, and signed segregation declarations.

obligation: Provide new clients with a clear option to choose either advisory or distribution services within the group/family at the time of onboarding.
evidence_required: Client onboarding selection form or digital audit log.

obligation: Use the Permanent Account Number (PAN) of each client as the unique control record for identification and client-level segregation.
evidence_required: Verified client PAN records database.

obligation: Reckon the family of an individual client as a single client and use the PAN of all family members jointly and severally as the control record.
evidence_required: Family declarations and mapped PAN record system.

obligation: Obtain an annual declaration or periodic update from the client in respect of dependent family members whose assets originate from the earning individual client.
evidence_required: Signed annual declaration form from the client.

obligation: Advise direct plans (non-commission based) of products only, wherever such plans are available.
evidence_required: Investment recommendation records and advisory logs.

obligation: Obtain an annual compliance audit certificate from an auditor confirming compliance with client level segregation requirements.
deadline: Within 6 months of the end of the financial year
evidence_required: Annual compliance audit report and auditor's segregation certificate.

obligation: Ensure that institutional clients and accredited investors sign a standard waiver to remain exempt from segregation requirements.
deadline: At onboarding of exclusive institutional/accredited clients
evidence_required: Executed standard waiver document.

obligation: Enter into an investment advisory agreement with clients incorporating the mandatory terms in Annexure A and the standardized MITC in Annexure B.
deadline: Prior to rendering advice or charging fees (By June 30, 2025 for existing clients as of Feb 17, 2025)
evidence_required: Executed client agreement or email transmission proof for existing clients.

obligation: Obtain client agreement signatures in person or via other legally acceptable modes including DigiLocker-enabled Aadhaar based e-signatures.
deadline: Prior to rendering any advice or charging any fees
evidence_required: Digitally or physically signed client agreements.

obligation: Provide clear guidance to clients in the agreement on the optional Centralised Fee Collection Mechanism (CeFCoM).
deadline: Prior to rendering advice or charging fees
evidence_required: Advisory agreement containing CeFCoM guidance clauses.

obligation: Limit fees for individual and HUF clients to either Assets under Advice (AUA) mode (capped at 2.5% of AUA p.a. per family of client) or Fixed fee mode (capped at ₹ 1,51,000 p.a. per family of client).
evidence_required: Client fee invoices, ledger, and agreement fee schedules.

obligation: Ensure that for second opinions on pre-existing distributed assets, the AUA fee does not exceed 2.5% p.a., and disclose and seek annual client consent regarding distributor fees.
evidence_required: Written disclosure copy and client consent record showing distributor consideration acknowledgement.

obligation: Limit advance fee collection to a maximum of one year.
evidence_required: Billing receipt logs, invoice dates, and client ledgers.

obligation: Maintain a baseline security deposit in the form of scheduled bank deposit (lien marked to IAASB) or units of liquid/overnight mutual funds based on client tiers: ₹ 1L (up to 150 clients), ₹ 2L (151-300), ₹ 5L (301-1000), or ₹ 10L (1001+).
deadline: Revised by April 30th of subsequent financial year (Existing IAs comply by Sept 30, 2025)
evidence_required: Bank lien letter or liquid/overnight MF unit statements marked lien to IAASB.

obligation: Comply with qualification and certification criteria, ensuring graduates or equivalent with NISM/accredited certs are eligible to act as IAs, or NISM PG holders renew certs within 3 years.
evidence_required: Degree certificates, NISM certifications, and renewal trackers.

obligation: Apply for in-principle registration as a non-individual IA if client count exceeds 300 at any point of time or financial year fees collected exceed ₹ 3 crores.
deadline: Within 3 months of reaching the threshold, completing transition within 3 months of in-principle approval
evidence_required: In-principle application on SI Portal and registration filings.

obligation: For part-time IAs, ensure investment advisory services are clearly segregated from other activities at all stages, and maintain arms-length relationships.
evidence_required: Separate operating manuals, segregation audit logs, and signed undertakings.

obligation: For part-time IAs, provide a prominent disclaimer (minimum 10 font size) on invoices indicating services are not under SEBI purview and no complaint can be raised to SEBI.
deadline: At time of billing non-SEBI services
evidence_required: Invoices showing mandatory SEBI disclaimer text.

obligation: Ensure partnership firm IAs apply for registration as LLPs or body corporates if no partner meets minimum qualification requirements.
evidence_required: Registration transformation applications submitted to MCA/SEBI.

obligation: For products and services not under SEBI, disclose and obtain appropriate signed client undertakings declaring that no SEBI grievance recourse is available.
deadline: At client onboarding (By July 31, 2025 for existing clients as of Jan 8, 2025)
evidence_required: Executed standard non-SEBI product disclosure and undertaking form.

obligation: Disclose to the client the extent of use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools in providing investment advice at the time of entering the agreement and as required.
deadline: At time of entering into agreement
evidence_required: Standard client agreements containing AI disclosures.

obligation: Maintain time-stamped call recordings of every consent for implementation/execution service obtained via telephone from advisory clients.
evidence_required: Time-stamped audio call logs linked to client execution ledger.

obligation: Conduct an annual audit of compliance with SEBI Regulations and specify compliance status on each provision in the annual audit report.
deadline: Within six months from the end of each financial year
evidence_required: Comprehensive annual compliance audit report specifying each provision.

obligation: Publish the compliance audit status and adverse findings, if any, along with action taken on the IA website and provide the report to clients.
deadline: Starting from audit report for financial year ending March 31, 2025
evidence_required: Website screenshots of audit reports and records of reports sent to clients.

obligation: Maintain a functional website containing details as specified by SEBI and confirm website details to IAASB.
deadline: By June 30, 2025 for existing IAs as on Jan 8, 2025
evidence_required: Active website domain and written confirmation receipt from IAASB.

obligation: Display updated investor complaints data on the homepage (without scrolling) of websites/mobile apps by the 7th of the succeeding month using font size 12 or above.
deadline: By the 7th of the succeeding month
evidence_required: Dated screenshots of the complaints table on the homepage.

obligation: Bring the updated SEBI Investor Charter to the notice of clients through website display, prominent office placement, and client onboarding kits.
evidence_required: Website charter screenshot, physical verification in offices, and copy of onboarding emails.

obligation: Ensure that any past performance or risk-return metric advertisements are verified by SEBI-recognized Past Risk and Return Verification Agency (PaRRVA).
evidence_required: Verification report from PaRRVA and approved advertisement layouts.

obligation: If communicating certified past performance prior to PaRRVA operationalization (on a 1-to-1 basis upon specific client request), attach the mandatory non-verification disclaimer.
deadline: During interim period prior to PaRRVA operationalization
evidence_required: Copies of certified performance sheets with the specified disclaimer.

obligation: Ensure mandatory compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 and its rules, specifically relating to digital accessibility guidelines of SEBI.
evidence_required: Digital accessibility compliance certificate or system testing reports.

obligation: Submit standardized periodic reports in the format specified in Annexure H on a half-yearly basis to IAASB within 30 days from period end.
deadline: Within 30 days of period ending Sept 30 and March 31
evidence_required: Filing trace and digital submission receipt from IAASB portal under Annexure H.
